steps.title: Identify the baseline principle
steps.content: Humans perceive dilated pupils positively and constricted pupils negatively — the principle the study tests on dogs.

steps.title: Apply the principle to the data
steps.content:
The table shows participants rated dogs with large-appearing pupils (light irises) more favorably than dogs with small-appearing pupils (dark irises) across all measured traits. This demonstrates the pupil-perception preference extends from people to dogs.

Choice C states this conclusion. The other options either deny any relationship exists (D), reverse the data (B), or make claims about actual size unsupported by the table (A).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
